content.inputFormat: First line contains T, the number of test cases. Each test case contains two lines. The first line contains two integers n and m, representing the sizes of arrays a and b respectively. The second line contains n integers representing the elements of array a. The third line contains m integers representing the elements of array b.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the expected number of inversions in array a after the described operations.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The expected number of inversions in the first test case is 13.833333 and in the second test case is 87.818182.
The expected number of inversions in this test case is 12.500000.
The expected number of inversions in this test case is 0.000000.

solution.approach: The problem can be solved by calculating the expected number of inversions in array a after the described operations. This can be done by iterating over each element in array a and calculating the probability that it will be greater than each element in array b after the merge operation.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * (n * m + n^2)) per test case, O(T * (n * m + n^2)) total.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(n + m) — for storing arrays a and b.
